description_long: This helmet features a rounded dome base adorned with a dense pattern of small, white cowrie shells meticulously attached across its surface. Two long, dark, spiraled horns rise symmetrically from the top, giving the helmet a striking and ceremonial appearance. A wide, reddish-brown leather band runs from the front to the back, bisecting the shell-covered dome and providing structural reinforcement. Leather straps, some with visible wear and fraying, hang from the sides and back, likely serving as retention or fastening elements. The overall construction suggests a blend of organic and crafted materials, with the horns and shells providing both decorative and symbolic value. The helmet's design is evocative of traditional or ritualistic headgear, possibly for ceremonial or cultural use.
description: A dome-shaped helmet covered in cowrie shells with two tall, spiraled horns and leather straps, featuring a central reddish-brown leather band.
description_view_0: Front view shows the symmetrical placement of the two horns, the central leather band, and the dense shell covering.
description_view_1: Angled top view highlights the height and curvature of the horns, as well as the shell pattern and the leather band running along the dome.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the leather band, the attachment points of the straps, and the continuation of the shell motif.
description_view_3: Angled rear view emphasizes the length of the horns, the shell texture, and the arrangement of the leather straps.
attribute_graphic_design: The helmet is decorated with a dense pattern of cowrie shells and a central reddish-brown leather band, with no additional graphic motifs or text.
